result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d make it much harder for human beings to dehumanize one another.

Not make everyone agree. Not erase conflict. Just change that reflex where a person becomes a category, a threat, a joke, a statistic, an enemy, a disposable body.

So much cruelty depends on that narrowing of vision. War, exploitation, abuse, neglect, corruption, prejudice — they all get easier when someone can stop seeing someone else as fully real.

If that changed, not everything would be fixed. People would still compete, misunderstand, fail, and hurt each other. But the floor would be higher. The worst things would be harder to justify. More people would hesitate at the crucial moment. More systems would have to account for the human cost they currently hide.

That’s the one change I’d pick: make recognition of another person’s humanity harder to lose.
